name: Customer Discovery Framework
purpose: Replace vague customer descriptions with testable segment hypotheses, observed problems, objections, and purchase evidence.
questionsToAsk: When did the customer last experience the problem?
What alternative did the customer use?
What did the current solution cost in money, time, or frustration?
What would make the customer switch or decline?
scoringModel: Score evidence recency, behavioral specificity, segment fit, sample coverage, and purchase evidence.
Treat demographics as unknown until supported by data.
Increase confidence when observations and purchases agree with interview themes.
Reduce confidence when the persona relies on stereotypes.
qualityChecks: Record customer language without inventing demographic details.
warnings: Stated interest can overestimate real purchasing behavior.
Customer personas are working hypotheses, not fixed truths.
